name: Type 1 Diabetes - Comprehensive Provider Query
description: Simulates an endocrinologist identifying their complete T1D patient population for an insulin pump or continuous glucose monitor initiative. T1D patients are highly identifiable: most have a formal diagnosis code, ALL are on insulin (lifelong), and most have intermittently elevated HbA1c. The challenge is distinguishing them from T2D patients who may also be on insulin (~25% of T2D requires insulin eventually).
prompt:
I need to identify my complete type 1 diabetes patient population for a continuous glucose monitor coverage initiative. From clinical experience, T1D patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal T1D diagnosis code, all are on insulin therapy (lifelong, from disease onset), and many have intermittently elevated HbA1c. Note that T2D patients may also be on insulin late in disease — distinguishing T1D from insulin-treated T2D requires the specific T1D diagnosis codes.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any T1D diagnosis (T1D parent, IDDM type IA, T1D with ulcer, pre-existing T1D, or T1D without retinopathy)
2. Patients prescribed insulin formulations
3. Patients with hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5 percent

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found. Note that the insulin and HbA1c queries will substantially overlap with T2D — the T1D diagnosis query is the most specific.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Union of T1D dx codes, insulin Rx, and elevated HbA1c. Tests subtype distinction (T1D specifically, not T2D) when the medication and lab queries are non-specific.

required_codes.display: Insulin dependent diabetes mellitus type IA
Type I diabetes mellitus with ulcer
